member.name: T:AasSchema.SampleUsage
member.summary:
The following code is intended to demonstrate the basics of using the Liquid XML Objects code you have just generated.
The creation of the sample code is an option within the XML Data Binder Wizard and can be turned off.

member.name: M:AasSchema.SampleUsage.SimpleXmlReader
member.summary: Demonstrates how to read XML data into a generated object. In this case the first element in your schema was selected.
member.remarks: LxSerializer.Deserialize has a number of other overloads allowing data to be read from a file/Stream/TextReader/XmlReader
member.exception: LxSerializer.Deserialize will throw if the XML data contains errors

member.name: M:AasSchema.SampleUsage.SimpleXmlWriter
member.summary: Demonstrates how to serialize a generated Liquid XML object to XML. In this case the first element in your schema was selected.
member.remarks: LxSerializer.Serialize has a number of other overloads allowing data to be written to a file/Stream/TextWriter/XmlWriter
member.exception: LxSerializer.Serialize will throw if the object being serialized contains errors

member.name: M:AasSchema.SampleUsage.ReadingXmlOfUnknownType
member.summary: Typically when you read an XML document you know what the root element should be, however in some instances the root element may not be known at design time. This sample demonstrates how to deal with this.
member.exception: LxSerializer.Deserialize will throw if the generated Liquid XML Objects library does not contain an object capable of having the XML data de-serialized into it.

member.name: M:AasSchema.SampleUsage.XmlReader_ErrorHandler(System.String,LiquidTechnologies.XmlObjects.LxErrorSeverity,LiquidTechnologies.XmlObjects.LxErrorCode,LiquidTechnologies.XmlObjects.TextLocation,System.Object)
member.summary: This method is called when an error or warning is reported during the de-serialization process. The method must throw an Exception in order to stop the de-serialization process. If it returns, the error/warning is ignored.
member.param: The error message describing the issue
The error code providing detail about the issue
The location of the issue in the source XML document
The object the data is being de-serialized into (a Liquid XML Objects generated class)

member.name: M:AasSchema.SampleUsage.XmlWriter_ErrorHandler(System.String,LiquidTechnologies.XmlObjects.LxErrorSeverity,LiquidTechnologies.XmlObjects.LxErrorCode,System.Object)
member.summary: This method is called when an error or warning is reported during the serialization process. The method must throw an Exception in order to stop the serialization process. If it returns, the error/warning is ignored.
member.param: The error message describing the issue
The error code providing detail about the issue
The object the data is being de-serialized into (a Liquid XML Objects generated class)

member.name: M:AasSchema.SampleUsage.ValidateUsingOriginalXmlSchema
member.summary: Uses the original XML Schemas to build a validator that can be used to validate the XML data directly or create a validating reader.
member.remarks: Note: AasValidator.Validate and AasValidator.CreateValidatingReader has a number of other overloads allowing data to be read from a file/Stream/TextReader/XmlReader

member.name: M:AasSchema.SampleUsage.ValidateUsingOriginalXmlSchema_ValidationEventHandler(System.Object,System.Xml.Schema.ValidationEventArgs)
member.summary: Called back when the AasValidator encounters validation errors and warnings
